title: Transfer Learning Across Seismic Domains: What Travels and What Doesn't
subtitle: Why a model trained in California needs retraining for Oklahoma — and how to do it efficiently
description: Transfer learning accelerates geophysical model development by reusing representations learned on large tectonic datasets. But domain shift between seismic regimes is real, and understanding what transfers is critical.
excerpt: Features learned on California tectonic seismicity do not map cleanly onto Oklahoma induced seismicity. Transfer learning helps — but only if you understand which layers to freeze and which to retrain.
hero_caption: Visualization of feature representations shifting between a source seismic domain and a target domain during transfer learning.
hero_aria: Abstract feature space visualization showing domain shift between tectonic and induced seismicity datasets
